metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the `submit` grant for `RebarBotNava` must be added to the **rebar project's own `refs/meta/config`** (i.e. `infra/gerrit/project.config` + `setup-project.sh`), NOT to All-Projects — because `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` on `refs/heads/*` in `project.config` eclipses any All-Projects inherited submit grant for this project.
findings.finding: The plan's AC instructs `setup-all-projects.sh` to grant `submit` on `refs/heads/*` via the All-Projects `refs/meta/config`, but the existing `infra/gerrit/project.config` already declares `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` on `refs/heads/*`. Under Gerrit semantics, `exclusiveGroupPermissions` causes the project to IGNORE any inherited All-Projects grant for matching refs — so an All-Projects submit grant for `RebarBotNava` would be silently eclipsed and have no effect for the `rebar` project. The submit permission must be added to the `rebar` project's own `refs/meta/config` (managed by `setup-project.sh` / `infra/gerrit/project.config`), not to All-Projects.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — Capability bullet: 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/*'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `RebarBotNava` to the `Contributors` group (already the default in `setup-project.sh` via `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS`) or add an explicit `submit = group Service Users` line under `[access "refs/heads/*"]` in `infra/gerrit/project.config`, and remove the submit-grant duty from `setup-all-projects.sh` scope (leaving that script to handle only the server-level `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` capability). Update `setup-all-projects.sh` Files entry accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the submit ACL in `setup-all-projects.sh`: since `rebar/project.config` uses `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`, any All-Projects submit grant is ignored for `rebar` — confirm whether the bot's `Contributors` membership (already set as the `setup-project.sh` default) is the correct + sufficient submit path, and either drop the All-Projects submit grant from `setup-all-projects.sh` scope or document why it is needed for a non-rebar project.
findings.finding: The plan's AC claims the bot needs a `submit` grant via `setup-all-projects.sh` on All-Projects `refs/heads/*` to avoid 403s on `POST /submit`, but this mechanism cannot work for the `rebar` project: `project.config` already sets `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` on `refs/heads/*`, which causes Gerrit to IGNORE any inherited All-Projects submit grant for that ref pattern. An All-Projects submit ACL is therefore a no-op for `rebar`. The real coverage comes from `RebarBotNava` being in the `Contributors` group (already the default in `setup-project.sh`: `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS="${CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS:-RebarBotNava}"`). The plan should either (a) drop the All-Projects submit grant (it does nothing for `rebar`) and rely on the existing Contributors membership, or (b) clarify that `setup-all-projects.sh` grants submit only on All-Projects as a belt-and-suspenders for other projects — but note this doesn't fix the `rebar` 403 path, which is already covered.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Capability bullet: 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/* (project.config restricts submit to Contributors+Administrators, so the bot must hold it or every POST /submit 403s)'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/*' clause from the `setup-all-projects.sh` scope (the bot already has submit via Contributors in the rebar project, and All-Projects submit is overridden by exclusiveGroupPermissions). The script's sole Gerrit-ACL work is the `[capability]` rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader grant. Add a comment noting that submit is covered by Contributors membership in setup-project.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify against Gerrit 3.14.1 docs/source that `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` can be granted as a `[capability]` entry in `All-Projects refs/meta/config`; if it is instead a project-level `[submit]` flag, update the plan (and `setup-all-projects.sh` spec) to set `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = true` in the `rebar` `project.config` `[submit]` section via `setup-project.sh`, and remove the capability-grant framing.
findings.finding: `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` is asserted to be a grantable server-level `[capability]` in `All-Projects refs/meta/config`, but this is an unverified capability assertion: in Gerrit 3.14.1 the documented global capabilities (administrateServer, runAs, streamEvents, etc.) do not include `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`. It is a project-level submit strategy setting (`[submit] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = true` in project.config), not a per-user capability grant — if the plan's mechanism is wrong, `setup-all-projects.sh` would silently no-op or be rejected by Gerrit, leaving the bot unable to rebase on behalf of uploaders.
findings.location: AC: Capability — '`rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` server-level capability (`All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` `[capability]`)' + Files: `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh` (NEW)
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/gerrit/project.config` under `[submit]`, add `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = true` (alongside the existing `action = fast forward only` or switching to `action = rebase if necessary` as appropriate for the auto-lander flow). Drop the `[capability]` grant from `setup-all-projects.sh`'s scope, since this is a per-project submit-strategy config, not a per-user server capability.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the All-Projects submit grant is actually needed given `rebar`'s `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` (which blocks inheritance) and that `RebarBotNava` is already a default member of `Contributors`; either drop the All-Projects submit grant from `setup-all-projects.sh`'s scope or document why it is needed for a project other than `rebar`.
findings.finding: The plan proposes granting `submit` on `refs/heads/*` in `All-Projects` for `RebarBotNava` via `setup-all-projects.sh`, but `rebar`'s existing `project.config` already has `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` which causes Gerrit to IGNORE the inherited All-Projects submit grant for `rebar` — making the All-Projects grant a no-op for this project. Additionally, `RebarBotNava` is already added to the `Contributors` group by `setup-project.sh` (default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS`), which already holds `submit` on `refs/heads/*` in the rebar project. The plan does not consider this prior art interaction before proposing a redundant/potentially-overbroad grant.
findings.location: AC: Capability — 'plus submit permission on `refs/heads/*`' + Files section: `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC: (1) `aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token --with-decryption --query Parameter.Type` returns `SecureString` and `grep -c AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN .env` returns 1; (2) `ssh gerrit gerrit capabilities --user RebarBotNava | grep rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`; (3) `curl -u RebarBotNava:<token> -X POST https://<gerrit>/a/changes/<id>/submit` returns HTTP 200.
findings.finding: None of the three acceptance criteria include a concrete proving command. AC1 (SSM/env) has no aws-cli or grep command; AC2 (capability grant) has no Gerrit SSH/REST query; AC3 (smoke) uses 'confirms' without specifying the command, target URL, or expected HTTP status — leaving done-ness unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet; all three AC checkboxes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare in the fetch-secrets.sh edit spec that `autolander-gerrit-token` MUST be fetched via `get_param` (required/fail-fast), not `get_param_optional`, so a missing or CHANGEME token aborts the boot rather than producing a silently broken `.env`.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare whether AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN should be fetched as *required* (fail-fast, like gerrit-bot-token) or *optional* (silent blank, like reviewbot-tickets-pat) in fetch-secrets.sh. If treated as optional, the autolander container will start with an empty credential and every Gerrit API call will 401 silently — an undeclared access posture on the container→Gerrit boundary.
findings.location: AC — Credential bullet / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh edit
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files / fetch-secrets.sh section, add: 'Use get_param (required, fail-fast), matching the treatment of gerrit-bot-token — the autolander cannot function without this credential so a CHANGEME or absent value must abort boot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure to setup-all-projects.sh (or its companion documentation) describing how to revert the All-Projects refs/meta/config capability grant (e.g. a paired `teardown-all-projects.sh` or a documented `git push` revert command).
findings.finding: The All-Projects refs/meta/config capability push (setup-all-projects.sh) has no explicit rollback or back-out plan. Modifying global Gerrit capabilities affects every project on the instance; if the grant needs to be reverted there is no described mechanism to do so.
findings.location: AC#2 (Capability) / infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' section to the plan (and a comment block in setup-all-projects.sh) that describes: (1) clone All-Projects, (2) remove the [capability] block from project.config, (3) push back to refs/meta/config. Alternatively add a teardown script that inverts the grant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `setup-all-projects.sh` applies a fully declarative (fetch-modify-push overwrite) or surgical (additive-only) edit to `All-Projects refs/meta/config`, and if surgical, document how out-of-band capability revocations are detected/repaired (e.g., a drift check that exits non-zero when expected grants are absent).
findings.finding: setup-all-projects.sh's convergence model for All-Projects refs/meta/config is unspecified: the plan does not state whether the script performs a declarative overwrite (like setup-project.sh does for the rebar project) or a surgical/additive edit. If surgical, a re-run after an out-of-band capability revocation will be a no-op (idempotent but NOT convergent), leaving the system in a bad state silently.
findings.location: Files section / AC: Capability — infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh (NEW)
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the setup-project.sh pattern: the script should fetch refs/meta/config of All-Projects, overwrite only the [capability] section (or the entire file if the project owns it declaratively), compute a diff, and push only if changes are needed — making it both idempotent AND convergent. Add a DRY_RUN=1 mode consistent with setup-project.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the smoke-test target: name a dedicated test repository/branch and a pre-created synthetic change (or document the steps to create one) so the submit exercise is isolated and repeatable without risk to production branches.
findings.finding: AC3's 'bot can authenticate + submit a test change on the instance' does not specify what test change is used (pre-existing change ID, synthetically created change, specific repo), making the smoke-test non-deterministic and potentially environment-destructive if the wrong change is submitted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'the bot can authenticate + submit a test change on the instance'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the idempotency mechanism (e.g., 'fetch refs/meta/config, check if the capability stanza already exists, only push if it differs') and add a proving command such as 'run the script twice; assert exit 0 and no new commit on refs/meta/config the second time'.
findings.finding: The idempotency requirement for setup-all-projects.sh ('re-run is a no-op') is stated as an AC but the plan specifies no mechanism (check-before-write, Gerrit API query, git diff before push) nor a proving command (e.g., run twice and assert no second commit/push occurs). An executor must invent both the implementation and the verification approach.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'setup-all-projects.sh is idempotent (re-run is a no-op)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Tests AC: 'Idempotency is implemented by fetching refs/meta/config, diffing the target stanza, and skipping the push if unchanged. Proved by: running the script twice in sequence and asserting `git log --oneline refs/meta/config` shows the same tip commit after both runs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly which refs/meta/config target receives the submit permission grant (All-Projects for global or the rebar project config for project-scoped) and update the script name/description accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan states the bot needs 'submit permission on refs/heads/*' but does not specify whether this grant belongs in All-Projects refs/meta/config (global) or in the rebar project's own refs/meta/config — two different config targets with different blast radii. An executing agent cannot determine where to write the stanza without guessing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Capability bullet: 'plus submit permission on refs/heads/*'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'The submit permission for `refs/heads/*` is written into the **rebar project's** `refs/meta/config` (not All-Projects), so `setup-all-projects.sh` handles only the `[capability]` stanza; `setup-project.sh` (or a new `setup-rebar-submit.sh`) adds the submit label entry.' Or if intentionally global, state: 'Submit is granted in All-Projects — this is intentional and scoped only to `refs/heads/*`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the idempotency detection rule for setup-all-projects.sh (e.g., 'grep for existing [capability] section before patching') and define the fallback/rollback behavior if the refs/meta/config push fails mid-run.
findings.finding: The idempotency protocol for setup-all-projects.sh is referenced ('re-run is a no-op') but its decision rule — specifically how the script detects an already-applied capability grant and what it does if the refs/meta/config fetch fails — is never defined. This is a processing protocol referenced without the accompanying logic specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – Tests bullet; Files – infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh (NEW)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Files or AC section: 'Script checks for existing RebarBotNava capability entry via grep on the fetched project.config; skips write if present. On push failure, restores the pre-edit config and exits non-zero with a descriptive message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback/teardown note to `setup-all-projects.sh` (or the AC) stating how to revoke the `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` capability grant and the `refs/heads/*` submit permission — matching the 'Back-out:' convention used in `setup-project.sh` and `project.config`.
findings.finding: The plan provides no rollback/teardown procedure for the All-Projects capability grant. The script is described as 'admin-only operator cutover' but the AC and Files sections contain no 'back-out' instruction (unlike setup-project.sh which documents 'delete these three submit lines and re-run' for every ACL it manages).
findings.location: Files section: infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh; AC: Capability bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In setup-all-projects.sh, add a comment block: 'Back-out: remove the [capability] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = group <RebarBotNava-group> line from All-Projects refs/meta/config and push; or set REVOKE=1 to have this script remove the grant and push the diff.' Mirror the DRY_RUN pattern from setup-project.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'RebarBotNava' as the canonical service-user name in the Files section description of setup-all-projects.sh so the account identifier is consistent across all sections.
findings.finding: The service-user identifier 'RebarBotNava' appears only in AC#2 but the Files section describing setup-all-projects.sh refers to it only as 'the autolander bot', creating a terminology drift that could cause an implementer to use the wrong account name in the script.
findings.location: Files section (setup-all-projects.sh description) vs AC#2
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files section, change 'admin-only provisioning of the All-Projects refs/meta/config [capability] grant' to 'admin-only provisioning of the All-Projects refs/meta/config [capability] grant for service user RebarBotNava'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh` to the `## Files` edit list for `infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md` (step 4), so operators know to run it during provisioning.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh` is a NEW file the plan creates. The `infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md` runbook (step 4) enumerates which `infra/gerrit/` scripts to run during provisioning/recovery, but does NOT enumerate `setup-all-projects.sh`. If an operator re-provisions from scratch following the runbook, the All-Projects capability grant will be skipped and the autolander will lack `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`.
findings.location: ## Files — `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh` (NEW); `infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md` step 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add `infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md` to the Files section as EDIT, appending a reference to `setup-all-projects.sh` in step 4 alongside the other `infra/gerrit/` scripts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note in setup-all-projects.sh's spec (or AC) acknowledging that the All-Projects submit ACL is server-wide and will need to be re-scoped (or an exclusiveGroupPermissions override added in each new project's config) if additional projects are ever added to the instance.
findings.finding: The All-Projects [capability] submit grant on refs/heads/* for RebarBotNava is server-wide: it applies to every project on the Gerrit instance, not only to the rebar project. The rebar project.config's exclusiveGroupPermissions=submit overrides this for rebar, but any future project added to the instance would inherit RebarBotNava's submit permission without an explicit opt-out — a broader-than-minimum grant.
findings.location: AC — Capability bullet / infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `fetch-secrets.sh` must exit non-zero (and log a clear error) if the SSM fetch for `autolander-gerrit-token` fails or returns the CHANGEME sentinel, preventing a silent partial `.env`.
findings.finding: fetch-secrets.sh has no stated error handling for SSM unavailability or a missing parameter: if the SSM call fails, the script may silently produce an incomplete `.env`, causing the autolander bot to start with a missing AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN and fail at runtime with no operator-visible signal.
findings.location: infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh / AC: Credential bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add `set -euo pipefail` to the script and validate each fetched value: if the returned value equals the CHANGEME sentinel or is empty, `echo "ERROR: autolander-gerrit-token not yet populated in SSM" >&2 && exit 1`. This surfaces the failure at deploy time rather than silently at bot runtime.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability plan for the new credential pipeline: at minimum, the bot should log a structured error (distinguishing 401/403 Gerrit responses) and a metric or alert should fire when submit attempts fail due to auth/capability errors.
findings.finding: No observability is planned for the new failure points introduced by the autolander credential pipeline: the SSM fetch, the Gerrit authentication, and the capability grant are all unmonitored — operators have no metric, log line, or alert to detect that the bot is missing its token or capability.
findings.location: AC: Tests bullet / overall plan

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the decision to use `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` + submit on `refs/heads/*` (rather than `Impersonate`) in a brief ADR or inline comment block at the top of `setup-all-projects.sh`, explaining the minimum-privilege rationale.
findings.finding: The architectural decision to grant `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` + `refs/heads/*` submit permission via `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` — rather than project-scoped permissions or broader `Impersonate` — is justified only inline in the plan and not captured in any persistent doc (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design note), making the rationale invisible to future operators who might 'simplify' it.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh / AC: Capability bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a header comment block to `setup-all-projects.sh` documenting: (1) why `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` is used instead of `Impersonate`, (2) why `All-Projects` scope is needed vs project-level, and (3) who must approve future changes to this grant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE section (or AC bullet) describing how to remove the SSM parameter from the `for_each` set and how to revoke the `All-Projects` capability/submit ACL (e.g., an inverse step in `setup-all-projects.sh` or a documented manual procedure).
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for the SSM parameter `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` or the `All-Projects` `[capability]` grant if the autolander bot is decommissioned — shared state accumulates with no removal specification.
findings.location: Files: `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh`; Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet: 'RETIRE: removing the bot requires (a) deleting the key from `local.rebar_secret_params` in `ssm.tf` and running `terraform apply`, and (b) running `setup-all-projects.sh --revoke` (or equivalent manual `git config` deletion) to remove the `[capability]` and submit ACL entries from `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config`.' Add a `--revoke` flag or note to `setup-all-projects.sh`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `fetch-secrets.sh` writes to a temp file and atomically renames it over `.env` (or documents that the script is invoked exclusively by a single caller), to prevent partial-write corruption under concurrent invocation.
findings.finding: The shared `.env` file is mutated by `fetch-secrets.sh` but the plan does not address concurrency safety if the script is invoked concurrently (e.g., two operators or two startup hooks running simultaneously), risking a partial/corrupt `.env`.
findings.location: Files: `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`; AC bullet 1 (`fetched by fetch-secrets.sh into the shared .env`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the `fetch-secrets.sh` edit description, note: 'write new content to `.env.tmp` then `mv -f .env.tmp .env` so concurrent runs are safe.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pointer (ticket link or file path) to the auto-lander bot's credential-consumption spec (env-var name, auth retry/fallback on 401/403) so the AC smoke-check pass criterion is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan references the 'auto-lander bot' agent and a distinct 'review-bot' as named actors whose agent definitions, decision rules, and retry/fallback protocols are never co-located with (or linked from) this provisioning plan — yet the AC ties credential materialization directly to bot authentication behavior ('bot can authenticate + submit a test change'). Because the agent's authentication flow, error handling on a 401/403, and the submit-retry logic are unspecified here, an executor cannot confirm the credential wiring is sufficient without hunting for those definitions elsewhere.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – Tests bullet; Files – fetch-secrets.sh mapping
findings.suggested_fix: In the Tests AC bullet, add: 'See <agent-spec file/ticket> for the bot's auth protocol; the smoke check is defined as: bot returns HTTP 200 on a dry-run submit within N retries, else fails with non-zero exit and a logged error.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
